/* set TRUE when a parallel connect attempt has\n                            started (happy eyeballs) */\n};\n\nstruct hostname {\n  char *rawalloc; /* allocated \"raw\" version of the name */\n  char *encalloc; /* allocated IDN-encoded version of the name */\n  char *name;     /* name to use internally, might be encoded, might be raw */\n  const char *dispname; /* name to display, as 'name' might be encoded */\n};\n\n/*\n * Flags on the keepon member of the Curl_transfer_keeper\n */\n\n#define KEEP_NONE  0\n#define KEEP_RECV  (1<<0)     /* there is or may be data to read */\n#define KEEP_SEND (1<<1)     /* there is or may be data to write */\n#define KEEP_RECV_HOLD (1<<2) /* when set, no reading should be done but there\n                                 might still be data to read */\n#define KEEP_SEND_HOLD (1<<3) /* when set, no writing should be done but there\n                                  might still be data to write */\n#define KEEP_RECV_PAUSE (1<<4) /* reading is paused */\n#define KEEP_SEND_PAUSE (1<<5) /* writing is paused */\n\n#define KEEP_RECVBITS (KEEP_RECV | KEEP_RECV_HOLD | KEEP_RECV_PAUSE)\n#define KEEP_SENDBITS (KEEP_SEND | KEEP_SEND_HOLD | KEEP_SEND_PAUSE)\n\n#if defined(CURLRES_ASYNCH) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_DOH)\n#define USE_CURL_ASYNC\nstruct Curl_async {\n  char *hostname;\n  struct Curl_dns_entry *dns;\n  struct thread_data *tdata;\n  void *resolver; /* resolver state, if it is used in the URL state -\n                     ares_channel e.g. */\n  int port;\n  int status; /* if done is TRUE, this is the status from the callback */\n  BIT(done);  /* set TRUE when the lookup is complete */\n};\n\n#endif\n\n#define FIRSTSOCKET     0\n#define SECONDARYSOCKET 1\n\nenum expect100 {\n  EXP100_SEND_DATA,           /* enough waiting, just send the body now */\n  EXP100_AWAITING_CONTINUE,   /* waiting for the 100 Continue header */\n  EXP100_SENDING_REQUEST,     /* still sending the request but will wait for\n                                 the 100 header once done with the request */\n  EXP100_FAILED               /* used on 417 Expectation Failed */\n};\n\nenum upgrade101 {\n  UPGR101_INIT,               /* default state */\n  UPGR101_WS,                 /* upgrade to WebSockets requested */\n  UPGR101_H2,                 /* upgrade to HTTP/2 requested */\n  UPGR101_RECEIVED,           /* 101 response received */\n  UPGR101_WORKING             /* talking upgraded protocol */\n};\n\nenum doh_slots {\n  /* Explicit values for first two symbols so as to match hard-coded\n   * constants in existing code\n   */\n  DOH_PROBE_SLOT_IPADDR_V4 = 0, /* make 'V4' stand out for readability */\n  DOH_PROBE_SLOT_IPADDR_V6 = 1, /* 'V6' likewise */\n\n  /* Space here for (possibly build-specific) additional slot definitions */\n\n  /* for example */\n  /* #ifdef WANT_DOH_FOOBAR_TXT */\n  /*   DOH_PROBE_SLOT_FOOBAR_TXT, */\n  /* #endif */\n\n  /* AFTER all slot definitions, establish how many we have */\n  DOH_PROBE_SLOTS\n};\n\n/*\n * Request specific data in the easy handle (Curl_easy).  Previously,\n * these members were on the connectdata struct but since a conn struct may\n * now be shared between different Curl_easys, we store connection-specific\n * data here. This struct only keeps stuff that's interesting for *this*\n * request, as it will be cleared between multiple ones\n */\nstruct SingleRequest {\n  curl_off_t size;        /* -1 if unknown at this point */\n  curl_off_t maxdownload; /* in bytes, the maximum amount of data to fetch,\n                             -1 means unlimited */\n  curl_off_t bytecount;         /* total number of bytes read */\n  curl_off_t writebytecount;    /* number of bytes written */\n\n  curl_off_t pendingheader;      /* this many bytes left to send is actually\n                                    header and not body */\n  struct curltime start;         /* transfer started at this time */\n  unsigned int headerbytecount;  /* received server headers (not CONNECT\n                                    headers) */\n  unsigned int allheadercount;   /* all received headers (server + CONNECT) */\n  unsigned int deductheadercount; /* this amount of bytes doesn't count when\n                                     we check if anything has been transferred\n                                     at the end of a connection. We use this\n                                     counter to make only a 100 reply (without\n                                     a following second response code) result\n                                     in a CURLE_GOT_NOTHING error code */\n  enum {\n    HEADER_NORMAL,              /* no bad header at all */\n    HEADER_PARTHEADER,          /* part of the chunk is a bad header, the rest\n                                   is normal data */\n    HEADER_ALLBAD               /* all was believed to be header */\n  } badheader;                  /* the header was deemed bad and will be\n                                   written as body */\n  int headerline;               /* counts header lines to better track the\n                                   first one */\n  char *str;                    /* within buf */\n  curl_off_t offset;            /* possible resume offset read from the\n                                   Content-Range: header */\n  int httpcode;                 /* error code from the 'HTTP/1.? XXX' or\n                                   'RTSP/1.? XXX' line */\n  int keepon;\n  struct curltime start100;      /* time stamp to wait for the 100 code from */\n  enum expect100 exp100;        /* expect 100 continue state */\n  enum upgrade101 upgr101;      /* 101 upgrade state */\n\n  /* Content unencoding stack. See sec 3.5, RFC2616. */\n  struct contenc_writer *writer_stack;\n  time_t timeofdoc;\n  long bodywrites;\n  char *location;   /* This points to an allocated version of the Location:\n                       header data */\n  char *newurl;     /* Set to the new URL to use when a redirect or a retry is\n                       wanted */\n\n  /* 'upload_present' is used to keep a byte counter of how much data there is\n     still left in the buffer, aimed for upload. */\n  ssize_t upload_present;\n\n  /* 'upload_fromhere' is used as a read-pointer when we uploaded parts of a\n     buffer, so the next read should read from where this pointer points to,\n     and the 'upload_present' contains the number of bytes available at this\n     position */\n  char *upload_fromhere;\n\n  /* Allocated protocol-specific data. Each protocol handler makes sure this\n     points to data it needs. */\n  union {\n    struct FILEPROTO *file;\n    struct FTP *ftp;\n    struct HTTP *http;\n    struct IMAP *imap;\n    struct ldapreqinfo *ldap;\n    struct MQTT *mqtt;\n    struct POP3 *pop3;\n    struct RTSP *rtsp;\n    struct smb_request *smb;\n    struct SMTP *smtp;\n    struct SSHPROTO *ssh;\n    struct TELNET *telnet;\n  } p;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  struct dohdata *doh; /* DoH specific data for this request */\n#endif\n#if defined(WIN32) && defined(USE_WINSOCK)\n  struct curltime last_sndbuf_update;  /* last time readwrite_upload called\n                                          win_update_buffer_size */\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES\n  unsigned char setcookies;\n#endif\n  unsigned char writer_stack_depth; /* Unencoding stack depth. */\n  BIT(header);        /* incoming data has HTTP header */\n  BIT(content_range); /* set TRUE if Content-Range: was found */\n  BIT(upload_done);   /* set to TRUE when doing chunked transfer-encoding\n                         upload and we're uploading the last chunk */\n  BIT(ignorebody);    /* we read a response-body but we ignore it! */\n  BIT(http_bodyless); /* HTTP response status code is between 100 and 199,\n                         204 or 304 */\n  BIT(chunk);         /* if set, this is a chunked transfer-encoding */\n  BIT(ignore_cl);     /* ignore content-length */\n  BIT(upload_chunky); /* set TRUE if we are doing chunked transfer-encoding\n                         on upload */\n  BIT(getheader);    /* TRUE if header parsing is wanted */\n  BIT(forbidchunk);  /* used only to explicitly forbid chunk-upload for\n                        specific upload buffers. See readmoredata() in http.c\n                        for details. */\n  BIT(no_body);      /* the response has no body */\n};\n\n/*\n * Specific protocol handler.\n */\n\nstruct Curl_handler {\n  const char *scheme;        /* URL scheme name. */\n\n  /* Complement to setup_connection_internals(). This is done before the\n     transfer \"owns\" the connection. */\n  CURLcode (*setup_connection)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                               struct connectdata *conn);\n\n  /* These two functions MUST be set to be protocol dependent */\n  CURLcode (*do_it)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\n  CURLcode (*done)(struct Curl_easy *, CURLcode, bool);\n\n  /* If the curl_do() function is better made in two halves, this\n   * curl_do_more() function will be called afterwards, if set. For example\n   * for doing the FTP stuff after the PASV/PORT command.\n   */\n  CURLcode (*do_more)(struct Curl_easy *, int *);\n\n  /* This function *MAY* be set to a protocol-dependent function that is run\n   * after the connect() and everything is done, as a step in the connection.\n   * The 'done' pointer points to a bool that should be set to TRUE if the\n   * function completes before return. If it doesn't complete, the caller\n   * should call the ->connecting() function until it is.\n   */\n  CURLcode (*connect_it)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\n\n  /* See above. */\n  CURLcode (*connecting)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\n  CURLcode (*doing)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\n\n  /* Called from the multi interface during the PROTOCONNECT phase, and it\n     should then return a proper fd set */\n  int (*proto_getsock)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                       struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);\n\n  /* Called from the multi interface during the DOING phase, and it should\n     then return a proper fd set */\n  int (*doing_getsock)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                       struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);\n\n  /* Called from the multi interface during the DO_MORE phase, and it should\n     then return a proper fd set */\n  int (*domore_getsock)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                        struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);\n\n  /* Called from the multi interface during the DO_DONE, PERFORM and\n     WAITPERFORM phases, and it should then return a proper fd set. Not setting\n     this will make libcurl use the generic default one. */\n  int (*perform_getsock)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                         struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);\n\n  /* This function *MAY* be set to a protocol-dependent function that is run\n   * by the curl_disconnect(), as a step in the disconnection.  If the handler\n   * is called because the connection has been considered dead,\n   * dead_connection is set to TRUE. The connection is (again) associated with\n   * the transfer here.\n   */\n  CURLcode (*disconnect)(struct Curl_easy *, struct connectdata *,\n                         bool dead_connection);\n\n  /* If used, this function gets called from transfer.c:readwrite_data() to\n     allow the protocol to do extra reads/writes */\n  CURLcode (*readwrite)(struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn,\n                        ssize_t *nread, bool *readmore);\n\n  /* This function can perform various checks on the connection. See\n     CONNCHECK_* for more information about the checks that can be performed,\n     and CONNRESULT_* for the results that can be returned. */\n  unsigned int (*connection_check)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                   struct connectdata *conn,\n                                   unsigned int checks_to_perform);\n\n  /* attach() attaches this transfer to this connection */\n  void (*attach)(struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn);\n\n  int defport;            /* Default port. */\n  curl_prot_t protocol;  /* See CURLPROTO_* - this needs to be the single\n                            specific protocol bit */\n  curl_prot_t family;    /* single bit for protocol family; basically the\n                            non-TLS name of the protocol this is */\n  unsigned int flags;     /* Extra particular characteristics, see PROTOPT_* */\n\n};\n\n#define PROTOPT_NONE 0             /* nothing extra */\n#define PROTOPT_SSL (1<<0)         /* uses SSL */\n#define PROTOPT_DUAL (1<<1)        /* this protocol uses two connections */\n#define PROTOPT_CLOSEACTION (1<<2) /* need action before socket close */\n/* some protocols will have to call the underlying functions without regard to\n   what exact state the socket signals. IE even if the socket says \"readable\",\n   the send function might need to be called while uploading, or vice versa.\n*/\n#define PROTOPT_DIRLOCK (1<<3)\n#define PROTOPT_NONETWORK (1<<4)   /* protocol doesn't use the network! */\n#define PROTOPT_NEEDSPWD (1<<5)    /* needs a password, and if none is set it\n                                      gets a default */\n#define PROTOPT_NOURLQUERY (1<<6)   /* protocol can't handle\n                                        url query strings (?foo=bar) ! */\n#define PROTOPT_CREDSPERREQUEST (1<<7) /* requires login credentials per\n                                          request instead of per connection */\n#define PROTOPT_ALPN (1<<8) /* set ALPN for this */\n/* (1<<9) was PROTOPT_STREAM, now free */\n#define PROTOPT_URLOPTIONS (1<<10) /* allow options part in the userinfo field\n                                      of the URL */\n#define PROTOPT_PROXY_AS_HTTP (1<<11) /* allow this non-HTTP scheme over a\n                                         HTTP proxy as HTTP proxies may know\n                                         this protocol and act as a gateway */\n#define PROTOPT_WILDCARD (1<<12) /* protocol supports wildcard matching */\n#define PROTOPT_USERPWDCTRL (1<<13) /* Allow \"control bytes\" (< 32 ascii) in\n                                       user name and password */\n#define PROTOPT_NOTCPPROXY (1<<14) /* this protocol can't proxy over TCP */\n\n#define CONNCHECK_NONE 0                 /* No checks */\n#define CONNCHECK_ISDEAD (1<<0)          /* Check if the connection is dead. */\n#define CONNCHECK_KEEPALIVE (1<<1)       /* Perform any keepalive function. */\n\n#define CONNRESULT_NONE 0                /* No extra information. */\n#define CONNRESULT_DEAD (1<<0)           /* The connection is dead. */\n\nstruct proxy_info {\n  struct hostname host;\n  int port;\n  unsigned char proxytype; /* curl_proxytype: what kind of proxy that is in\n                              use */\n  char *user;    /* proxy user name string, allocated */\n  char *passwd;  /* proxy password string, allocated */\n};\n\nstruct ldapconninfo;\n\n#define TRNSPRT_TCP 3\n#define TRNSPRT_UDP 4\n#define TRNSPRT_QUIC 5\n#define TRNSPRT_UNIX 6\n\n/*\n * The connectdata struct contains all fields and variables that should be\n * unique for an entire connection.\n */\nstruct connectdata {\n  struct Curl_llist_element bundle_node; /* conncache */\n\n  /* chunk is for HTTP chunked encoding, but is in the general connectdata\n     struct only because we can do just about any protocol through an HTTP\n     proxy and an HTTP proxy may in fact respond using chunked encoding */\n  struct Curl_chunker chunk;\n\n  curl_closesocket_callback fclosesocket; /* function closing the socket(s) */\n  void *closesocket_client;\n\n  /* This is used by the connection cache logic. If this returns TRUE, this\n     handle is still used by one or more easy handles and can only used by any\n     other easy handle without careful consideration (== only for\n     multiplexing) and it cannot be used by another multi handle! */\n#define CONN_INUSE(c) ((c)->easyq.size)\n\n  /**** Fields set when inited and not modified again */\n  curl_off_t connection_id; /* Contains a unique number to make it easier to\n                               track the connections in the log output */\n\n  /* 'dns_entry' is the particular host we use. This points to an entry in the\n     DNS cache and it will not get pruned while locked. It gets unlocked in\n     multi_done(). This entry will be NULL if the connection is reused as then\n     there is no name resolve done. */\n  struct Curl_dns_entry *dns_entry;\n\n  /* 'remote_addr' is the particular IP we connected to. it is owned, set\n   * and NULLed by the connected socket filter (if there is one). */\n  const struct Curl_sockaddr_ex *remote_addr;\n\n  struct hostname host;\n  char *hostname_resolve; /* host name to resolve to address, allocated */\n  char *secondaryhostname; /* secondary socket host name (ftp) */\n  struct hostname conn_to_host; /* the host to connect to. valid only if\n                                   bits.conn_to_host is set */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  struct proxy_info socks_proxy;\n  struct proxy_info http_proxy;\n#endif\n  /* 'primary_ip' and 'primary_port' get filled with peer's numerical\n     ip address and port number whenever an outgoing connection is\n     *attempted* from the primary socket to a remote address. When more\n     than one address is tried for a connection these will hold data\n     for the last attempt. When the connection is actually established\n     these are updated with data which comes directly from the socket. */\n\n  char primary_ip[MAX_IPADR_LEN];\n  char *user;    /* user name string, allocated */\n  char *passwd;  /* password string, allocated */\n  char *options; /* options string, allocated */\n  char *sasl_authzid;     /* authorization identity string, allocated */\n  char *oauth_bearer; /* OAUTH2 bearer, allocated */\n  struct curltime now;     /* \"current\" time */\n  struct curltime created; /* creation time */\n  struct curltime lastused; /* when returned to the connection cache */\n  curl_socket_t sock[2]; /* two sockets, the second is used for the data\n                            transfer when doing FTP */\n  Curl_recv *recv[2];\n  Curl_send *send[2];\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cfilter[2]; /* connection filters */\n\n  struct ssl_primary_config ssl_config;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  struct ssl_primary_config proxy_ssl_config;\n#endif\n  struct ConnectBits bits;    /* various state-flags for this connection */\n\n  const struct Curl_handler *handler; /* Connection's protocol handler */\n  const struct Curl_handler *given;   /* The protocol first given */\n\n  /* Protocols can use a custom keepalive mechanism to keep connections alive.\n     This allows those protocols to track the last time the keepalive mechanism\n     was used on this connection. */\n  struct curltime keepalive;\n\n  /**** curl_get() phase fields */\n\n  curl_socket_t sockfd;   /* socket to read from or CURL_SOCKET_BAD */\n  curl_socket_t writesockfd; /* socket to write to, it may very\n                                well be the same we read from.\n                                CURL_SOCKET_BAD disables */\n\n#ifdef HAVE_GSSAPI\n  BIT(sec_complete); /* if Kerberos is enabled for this connection */\n  unsigned char command_prot; /* enum protection_level */\n  unsigned char data_prot; /* enum protection_level */\n  unsigned char request_data_prot; /* enum protection_level */\n  size_t buffer_size;\n  struct krb5buffer in_buffer;\n  void *app_data;\n  const struct Curl_sec_client_mech *mech;\n  struct sockaddr_in local_addr;\n#endif\n\n#if defined(USE_KERBEROS5)    /* Consider moving some of the above GSS-API */\n  struct kerberos5data krb5;  /* variables into the structure definition, */\n#endif                        /* however, some of them are ftp specific. */\n\n  struct Curl_llist easyq;    /* List of easy handles using this connection */\n  curl_seek_callback seek_func; /* function that seeks the input */\n  void *seek_client;            /* pointer to pass to the seek() above */\n\n  /*************** Request - specific items ************/\n#if defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI) && defined(SECPKG_ATTR_ENDPOINT_BINDINGS)\n  CtxtHandle *sslContext;\n#endif\n\n#ifdef USE_GSASL\n  struct gsasldata gsasl;\n#endif\n\n#if defined(USE_NTLM)\n  curlntlm http_ntlm_state;\n  curlntlm proxy_ntlm_state;\n\n  struct ntlmdata ntlm;     /* NTLM differs from other authentication schemes\n                               because it authenticates connections, not\n                               single requests! */\n  struct ntlmdata proxyntlm; /* NTLM data for proxy */\n#endif\n\n#ifdef USE_SPNEGO\n  curlnegotiate http_negotiate_state;\n  curlnegotiate proxy_negotiate_state;\n\n  struct negotiatedata negotiate; /* state data for host Negotiate auth */\n  struct negotiatedata proxyneg; /* state data for proxy Negotiate auth */\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n  /* for chunked-encoded trailer */\n  struct dynbuf trailer;\n#endif\n\n  union {\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n    struct ftp_conn ftpc;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_SSH\n    struct ssh_conn sshc;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_TFTP\n    struct tftp_state_data *tftpc;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_IMAP\n    struct imap_conn imapc;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_POP3\n    struct pop3_conn pop3c;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_SMTP\n    struct smtp_conn smtpc;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_RTSP\n    struct rtsp_conn rtspc;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_SMB\n    struct smb_conn smbc;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_LIBRTMP\n    void *rtmp;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_OPENLDAP\n    struct ldapconninfo *ldapc;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_MQTT\n    struct mqtt_conn mqtt;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_WEBSOCKETS\n    struct websocket *ws;\n#endif\n    unsigned int unused:1; /* avoids empty union */\n  } proto;\n\n  struct connectbundle *bundle; /* The bundle we are member of */\n#ifdef USE_UNIX_SOCKETS\n  char *unix_domain_socket;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_HYPER\n  /* if set, an alternative data transfer function */\n  Curl_datastream datastream;\n#endif\n  /* When this connection is created, store the conditions for the local end\n     bind. This is stored before the actual bind and before any connection is\n     made and will serve the purpose of being used for comparison reasons so\n     that subsequent bound-requested connections aren't accidentally reusing\n     wrong connections. */\n  char *localdev;\n  unsigned short localportrange;\n  int waitfor;      /* current READ/WRITE bits to wait for */\n#if defined(HAVE_GSSAPI) || defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI)\n  int socks5_gssapi_enctype;\n#endif\n  /* The field below gets set in connect.c:connecthost() */\n  int port;        /* which port to use locally - to connect to */\n  int remote_port; /* the remote port, not the proxy port! */\n  int conn_to_port; /* the remote port to connect to. valid only if\n                       bits.conn_to_port is set */\n#ifdef ENABLE_IPV6\n  unsigned int scope_id;  /* Scope id for IPv6 */\n#endif\n  unsigned short localport;\n  unsigned short secondary_port; /* secondary socket remote port to connect to\n                                    (ftp) */\n  unsigned char cselect_bits; /* bitmask of socket events */\n  unsigned char alpn; /* APLN TLS negotiated protocol, a CURL_HTTP_VERSION*\n                         value */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  unsigned char proxy_alpn; /* APLN of proxy tunnel, CURL_HTTP_VERSION* */\n#endif\n  unsigned char transport; /* one of the TRNSPRT_* defines */\n  unsigned char ip_version; /* copied from the Curl_easy at creation time */\n  unsigned char httpversion; /* the HTTP version*10 reported by the server */\n  unsigned char connect_only;\n  unsigned char gssapi_delegation; /* inherited from set.gssapi_delegation */\n};\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n#define CURL_CONN_HOST_DISPNAME(c) \\\n          ((c)->bits.socksproxy ? (c)->socks_proxy.host.dispname : \\\n            (c)->bits.httpproxy ? (c)->http_proxy.host.dispname : \\\n              (c)->bits.conn_to_host ? (c)->conn_to_host.dispname : \\\n                (c)->host.dispname)\n#else\n#define CURL_CONN_HOST_DISPNAME(c) \\\n          (c)->bits.conn_to_host ? (c)->conn_to_host.dispname : \\\n            (c)->host.dispname\n#endif\n\n/* The end of connectdata. */\n\n/*\n * Struct to keep statistical and informational data.\n * All variables in this struct must be initialized/reset in Curl_initinfo().\n */\nstruct PureInfo {\n  int httpcode;  /* Recent HTTP, FTP, RTSP or SMTP response code */\n  int httpproxycode; /* response code from proxy when received separate */\n  int httpversion; /* the http version number X.Y = X*10+Y */\n  time_t filetime; /* If requested, this is might get set. Set to -1 if the\n                      time was unretrievable. */\n  curl_off_t request_size; /* the amount of bytes sent in the request(s) */\n  unsigned long proxyauthavail; /* what proxy auth types were announced */\n  unsigned long httpauthavail;  /* what host auth types were announced */\n  long numconnects; /* how many new connection did libcurl created */\n  char *contenttype; /* the content type of the object */\n  char *wouldredirect; /* URL this would've been redirected to if asked to */\n  curl_off_t retry_after; /* info from Retry-After: header */\n  unsigned int header_size;  /* size of read header(s) in bytes */\n\n  /* PureInfo members 'conn_primary_ip', 'conn_primary_port', 'conn_local_ip'\n     and, 'conn_local_port' are copied over from the connectdata struct in\n     order to allow curl_easy_getinfo() to return this information even when\n     the session handle is no longer associated with a connection, and also\n     allow curl_easy_reset() to clear this information from the session handle\n     without disturbing information which is still alive, and that might be\n     reused, in the connection cache. */\n\n  char conn_primary_ip[MAX_IPADR_LEN];\n  int conn_primary_port; /* this is the destination port to the connection,\n                            which might have been a proxy */\n  int conn_remote_port;  /* this is the \"remote port\", which is the port\n                            number of the used URL, independent of proxy or\n                            not */\n  char conn_local_ip[MAX_IPADR_LEN];\n  int conn_local_port;\n  const char *conn_scheme;\n  unsigned int conn_protocol;\n  struct curl_certinfo certs; /* info about the certs. Asked for with\n                                 CURLOPT_CERTINFO / CURLINFO_CERTINFO */\n  CURLproxycode pxcode;\n  BIT(timecond);  /* set to TRUE if the time condition didn't match, which\n                     thus made the document NOT get fetched */\n};\n\n\nstruct Progress {\n  time_t lastshow; /* time() of the last displayed progress meter or NULL to\n                      force redraw at next call */\n  curl_off_t size_dl; /* total expected size */\n  curl_off_t size_ul; /* total expected size */\n  curl_off_t downloaded; /* transferred so far */\n  curl_off_t uploaded; /* transferred so far */\n\n  curl_off_t current_speed; /* uses the currently fastest transfer */\n\n  int width; /* screen width at download start */\n  int flags; /* see progress.h */\n\n  timediff_t timespent;\n\n  curl_off_t dlspeed;\n  curl_off_t ulspeed;\n\n  timediff_t t_nslookup;\n  timediff_t t_connect;\n  timediff_t t_appconnect;\n  timediff_t t_pretransfer;\n  timediff_t t_starttransfer;\n  timediff_t t_redirect;\n\n  struct curltime start;\n  struct curltime t_startsingle;\n  struct curltime t_startop;\n  struct curltime t_acceptdata;\n\n\n  /* upload speed limit */\n  struct curltime ul_limit_start;\n  curl_off_t ul_limit_size;\n  /* download speed limit */\n  struct curltime dl_limit_start;\n  curl_off_t dl_limit_size;\n\n#define CURR_TIME (5 + 1) /* 6 entries for 5 seconds */\n\n  curl_off_t speeder[ CURR_TIME ];\n  struct curltime speeder_time[ CURR_TIME ];\n  int speeder_c;\n  BIT(callback);  /* set when progress callback is used */\n  BIT(is_t_startransfer_set);\n};\n\ntypedef enum {\n    RTSPREQ_NONE, /* first in list */\n    RTSPREQ_OPTIONS,\n    RTSPREQ_DESCRIBE,\n    RTSPREQ_ANNOUNCE,\n    RTSPREQ_SETUP,\n    RTSPREQ_PLAY,\n    RTSPREQ_PAUSE,\n    RTSPREQ_TEARDOWN,\n    RTSPREQ_GET_PARAMETER,\n    RTSPREQ_SET_PARAMETER,\n    RTSPREQ_RECORD,\n    RTSPREQ_RECEIVE,\n    RTSPREQ_LAST /* last in list */\n} Curl_RtspReq;\n\nstruct auth {\n  unsigned long want;  /* Bitmask set to the authentication methods wanted by\n                          app (with CURLOPT_HTTPAUTH or CURLOPT_PROXYAUTH). */\n  unsigned long picked;\n  unsigned long avail; /* Bitmask for what the server reports to support for\n                          this resource */\n  BIT(done); /* TRUE if digest should be done IE-style or FALSE if it\n                   should be RFC compliant */\n};\n\n#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\nstruct Curl_data_prio_node {\n  struct Curl_data_prio_node *next;\n  struct Curl_easy *data;\n};\n#endif\n\n/**\n * Priority information for an easy handle in relation to others\n * on the same connection.\n * TODO: we need to adapt it to the new priority scheme as defined in RFC 9218\n */\nstruct Curl_data_priority {\n#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\n  /* tree like dependencies only implemented in nghttp2 */\n  struct Curl_easy *parent;\n  struct Curl_data_prio_node *children;\n#endif\n  int weight;\n#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\n  BIT(exclusive);\n#endif\n};\n\n/*\n * This struct is for holding data that was attempted to get sent to the user's\n * callback but is held due to pausing. One instance per type (BOTH, HEADER,\n * BODY).\n */\nstruct tempbuf {\n  struct dynbuf b;\n  int type;   /* type of the 'tempwrite' buffer as a bitmask that is used with\n                 Curl_client_write() */\n  BIT(paused_body); /* if PAUSE happened before/during BODY write */\n};\n\n/* Timers */\ntypedef enum {\n  EXPIRE_100_TIMEOUT,\n  EXPIRE_ASYNC_NAME,\n  EXPIRE_CONNECTTIMEOUT,\n  EXPIRE_DNS_PER_NAME, /* family1 */\n  EXPIRE_DNS_PER_NAME2, /* family2 */\n  EXPIRE_HAPPY_EYEBALLS_DNS, /* See asyn-ares.c */\n  EXPIRE_HAPPY_EYEBALLS,\n  EXPIRE_MULTI_PENDING,\n  EXPIRE_RUN_NOW,\n  EXPIRE_SPEEDCHECK,\n  EXPIRE_TIMEOUT,\n  EXPIRE_TOOFAST,\n  EXPIRE_QUIC,\n  EXPIRE_FTP_ACCEPT,\n  EXPIRE_ALPN_EYEBALLS,\n  EXPIRE_LAST /* not an actual timer, used as a marker only */\n} expire_id;\n\n\ntypedef enum {\n  TRAILERS_NONE,\n  TRAILERS_INITIALIZED,\n  TRAILERS_SENDING,\n  TRAILERS_DONE\n} trailers_state;\n\n\n/*\n * One instance for each timeout an easy handle can set.\n */\nstruct time_node {\n  struct Curl_llist_element list;\n  struct curltime time;\n  expire_id eid;\n};\n\n/* individual pieces of the URL */\nstruct urlpieces {\n  char *scheme;\n  char *hostname;\n  char *port;\n  char *user;\n  char *password;\n  char *options;\n  char *path;\n  char *query;\n};\n\nstruct UrlState {\n  /* Points to the connection cache */\n  struct conncache *conn_cache;\n  /* buffers to store authentication data in, as parsed from input options */\n  struct curltime keeps_speed; /* set TRUE when a parallel connect attempt has\n                            started (happy eyeballs) */\n};\n\nstruct hostname {\n  char *rawalloc; /* allocated \"raw\" version of the name */\n  char *encalloc; /* allocated IDN-encoded version of the name */\n  char *name;     /* name to use internally, might be encoded, might be raw */\n  const char *dispname; /* name to display, as 'name' might be encoded */\n};\n\n/*\n * Flags on the keepon member of the Curl_transfer_keeper\n */\n\n#define KEEP_NONE  0\n#define KEEP_RECV  (1<<0)     /* there is or may be data to read */\n#define KEEP_SEND (1<<1)     /* there is or may be data to write */\n#define KEEP_RECV_HOLD (1<<2) /* when set, no reading should be done but there\n                                 might still be data to read */\n#define KEEP_SEND_HOLD (1<<3) /* when set, no writing should be done but there\n                                  might still be data to write */\n#define KEEP_RECV_PAUSE (1<<4) /* reading is paused */\n#define KEEP_SEND_PAUSE (1<<5) /* writing is paused */\n\n#define KEEP_RECVBITS (KEEP_RECV | KEEP_RECV_HOLD | KEEP_RECV_PAUSE)\n#define KEEP_SENDBITS (KEEP_SEND | KEEP_SEND_HOLD | KEEP_SEND_PAUSE)\n\n#if defined(CURLRES_ASYNCH) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_DOH)\n#define USE_CURL_ASYNC\nstruct Curl_async {\n  char *hostname;\n  struct Curl_dns_entry *dns;\n  struct thread_data *tdata;\n  void *resolver; /* resolver state, if it is used in the URL state -\n                     ares_channel e.g. */\n  int port;\n  int status; /* if done is TRUE, this is the status from the callback */\n  BIT(done);  /* set TRUE when the lookup is complete */\n};\n\n#endif\n\n#define FIRSTSOCKET     0\n#define SECONDARYSOCKET 1\n\nenum expect100 {\n  EXP100_SEND_DATA,           /* enough waiting, just send the body now */\n  EXP100_AWAITING_CONTINUE,   /* waiting for the 100 Continue header */\n  EXP100_SENDING_REQUEST,     /* still sending the request but will wait for\n                                 the 100 header once done with the request */\n  EXP100_FAILED               /* used on 417 Expectation Failed */\n};\n\nenum upgrade101 {\n  UPGR101_INIT,               /* default state */\n  UPGR101_WS,                 /* upgrade to WebSockets requested */\n  UPGR101_H2,                 /* upgrade to HTTP/2 requested */\n  UPGR101_RECEIVED,           /* 101 response received */\n  UPGR101_WORKING             /* talking upgraded protocol */\n};\n\nenum doh_slots {\n  /* Explicit values for first two symbols so as to match hard-coded\n   * constants in existing code\n   */\n  DOH_PROBE_SLOT_IPADDR_V4 = 0, /* make 'V4' stand out for readability */\n  DOH_PROBE_SLOT_IPADDR_V6 = 1, /* 'V6' likewise */\n\n  /* Space here for (possibly build-specific) additional slot definitions */\n\n  /* for example */\n  /* #ifdef WANT_DOH_FOOBAR_TXT */\n  /*   DOH_PROBE_SLOT_FOOBAR_TXT, */\n  /* #endif */\n\n  /* AFTER all slot definitions, establish how many we have */\n  DOH_PROBE_SLOTS\n};\n\n/*\n * Request specific data in the easy handle (Curl_easy).  Previously,\n * these members were on the connectdata struct but since a conn struct may\n * now be shared between different Curl_easys, we store connection-specific\n * data here. This struct only keeps stuff that's interesting for *this*\n * request, as it will be cleared between multiple ones\n */\nstruct SingleRequest {\n  curl_off_t size;        /* -1 if unknown at this point */\n  curl_off_t maxdownload; /* in bytes, the maximum amount of data to fetch,\n                             -1 means unlimited */\n  curl_off_t bytecount;         /* total number of bytes read */\n  curl_off_t writebytecount;    /* number of bytes written */\n\n  curl_off_t pendingheader;      /* this many bytes left to send is actually\n                                    header and not body */\n  struct curltime start;         /* transfer started at this time */\n  unsigned int headerbytecount;  /* received server headers (not CONNECT\n                                    headers) */\n  unsigned int allheadercount;   /* all received headers (server + CONNECT) */\n  unsigned int deductheadercount; /* this amount of bytes doesn't count when\n                                     we check if anything has been transferred\n                                     at the end of a connection. We use this\n                                     counter to make only a 100 reply (without\n                                     a following second response code) result\n                                     in a CURLE_GOT_NOTHING error code */\n  enum {\n    HEADER_NORMAL,              /* no bad header at all */\n    HEADER_PARTHEADER,          /* part of the chunk is a bad header, the rest\n                                   is normal data */\n    HEADER_ALLBAD               /* all was believed to be header */\n  } badheader;                  /* the header was deemed bad and will be\n                                   written as body */\n  int headerline;               /* counts header lines to better track the\n                                   first one */\n  char *str;                    /* within buf */\n  curl_off_t offset;            /* possible resume offset read from the\n                                   Content-Range: header */\n  int httpcode;                 /* error code from the 'HTTP/1.? XXX' or\n                                   'RTSP/1.? XXX' line */\n  int keepon;\n  struct curltime start100;      /* time stamp to wait for the 100 code from */\n  enum expect100 exp100;        /* expect 100 continue state */\n  enum upgrade101 upgr101;      /* 101 upgrade state */\n\n  /* Content unencoding stack. See sec 3.5, RFC2616. */\n  struct contenc_writer *writer_stack;\n  time_t timeofdoc;\n  long bodywrites;\n  char *location;   /* This points to an allocated version of the Location:\n                       header data */\n  char *newurl;     /* Set to the new URL to use when a redirect or a retry is\n                       wanted */\n\n  /* 'upload_present' is used to keep a byte counter of how much data there is\n     still left in the buffer, aimed for upload. */\n  ssize_t upload_present;\n\n  /* 'upload_fromhere' is used as a read-pointer when we uploaded parts of a\n     buffer, so the next read should read from where this pointer points to,\n     and the 'upload_present' contains the number of bytes available at this\n     position */\n  char *upload_fromhere;\n\n  /* Allocated protocol-specific data. Each protocol handler makes sure this\n     points to data it needs. */\n  union {\n    struct FILEPROTO *file;\n    struct FTP *ftp;\n    struct HTTP *http;\n    struct IMAP *imap;\n    struct ldapreqinfo *ldap;\n    struct MQTT *mqtt;\n    struct POP3 *pop3;\n    struct RTSP *rtsp;\n    struct smb_request *smb;\n    struct SMTP *smtp;\n    struct SSHPROTO *ssh;\n    struct TELNET *telnet;\n  } p;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  struct dohdata *doh; /* DoH specific data for this request */\n#endif\n#if defined(WIN32) && defined(USE_WINSOCK)\n  struct curltime last_sndbuf_update;  /* last time readwrite_upload called\n                                          win_update_buffer_size */\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES\n  unsigned char setcookies;\n#endif\n  unsigned char writer_stack_depth; /* Unencoding stack depth. */\n  BIT(header);        /* incoming data has HTTP header */\n  BIT(content_range); /* set TRUE if Content-Range: was found */\n  BIT(upload_done);   /* set to TRUE when doing chunked transfer-encoding\n                         upload and we're uploading the last chunk */\n  BIT(ignorebody);    /* we read a response-body but we ignore it! */\n  BIT(http_bodyless); /* HTTP response status code is between 100 and 199,\n                         204 or 304 */\n  BIT(chunk);         /* if set, this is a chunked transfer-encoding */\n  BIT(ignore_cl);     /* ignore content-length */\n  BIT(upload_chunky); /* set TRUE if we are doing chunked transfer-encoding\n                         on upload */\n  BIT(getheader);    /* TRUE if header parsing is wanted */\n  BIT(forbidchunk);  /* used only to explicitly forbid chunk-upload for\n                        specific upload buffers. See readmoredata() in http.c\n                        for details. */\n  BIT(no_body);      /* the response has no body */\n};\n\n/*\n * Specific protocol handler.\n */\n\nstruct Curl_handler {\n  const char *scheme;        /* URL scheme name. */\n\n  /* Complement to setup_connection_internals(). This is done before the\n     transfer \"owns\" the connection. */\n  CURLcode (*setup_connection)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                               struct connectdata *conn);\n\n  /* These two functions MUST be set to be protocol dependent */\n  CURLcode (*do_it)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\n  CURLcode (*done)(struct Curl_easy *, CURLcode, bool);\n\n  /* If the curl_do() function is better made in two halves, this\n   * curl_do_more() function will be called afterwards, if set. For example\n   * for doing the FTP stuff after the PASV/PORT command.\n   */\n  CURLcode (*do_more)(struct Curl_easy *, int *);\n\n  /* This function *MAY* be set to a protocol-dependent function that is run\n   * after the connect() and everything is done, as a step in the connection.\n   * The 'done' pointer points to a bool that should be set to TRUE if the\n   * function completes before return. If it doesn't complete, the caller\n   * should call the ->connecting() function until it is.\n   */\n  CURLcode (*connect_it)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\n\n  /* See above. */\n  CURLcode (*connecting)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\n  CURLcode (*doing)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\n\n  /* Called from the multi interface during the PROTOCONNECT phase, and it\n     should then return a proper fd set */\n  int (*proto_getsock)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                       struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);\n\n  /* Called from the multi interface during the DOING phase, and it should\n     then return a proper fd set */\n  int (*doing_getsock)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                       struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);\n\n  /* Called from the multi interface during the DO_MORE phase, and it should\n     then return a proper fd set */\n  int (*domore_getsock)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                        struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);\n\n  /* Called from the multi interface during the DO_DONE, PERFORM and\n     WAITPERFORM phases, and it should then return a proper fd set. Not setting\n     this will make libcurl use the generic default one. */\n  int (*perform_getsock)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                         struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);\n\n  /* This function *MAY* be set to a protocol-dependent function that is run\n   * by the curl_disconnect(), as a step in the disconnection.  If the handler\n   * is called because the connection has been considered dead,\n   * dead_connection is set to TRUE. The connection is (again) associated with\n   * the transfer here.\n   */\n  CURLcode (*disconnect)(struct Curl_easy *, struct connectdata *,\n                         bool dead_connection);\n\n  /* If used, this function gets called from transfer.c:readwrite_data() to\n     allow the protocol to do extra reads/writes */\n  CURLcode (*readwrite)(struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn,\n                        ssize_t *nread, bool *readmore);\n\n  /* This function can perform various checks on the connection. See\n     CONNCHECK_* for more information about the checks that can be performed,\n     and CONNRESULT_* for the results that can be returned. */\n  unsigned int (*connection_check)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                   struct connectdata *conn,\n                                   unsigned int checks_to_perform);\n\n  /* attach() attaches this transfer to this connection */\n  void (*attach)(struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn);\n\n  int defport;            /* Default port. */\n  curl_prot_t protocol;  /* See CURLPROTO_* - this needs to be the single\n                            specific protocol bit */\n  curl_prot_t family;    /* single bit for protocol family; basically the\n                            non-TLS name of the protocol this is */\n  unsigned int flags;     /* Extra particular characteristics, see PROTOPT_* */\n\n};\n\n#define PROTOPT_NONE 0             /* nothing extra */\n#define PROTOPT_SSL (1<<0)         /* uses SSL */\n#define PROTOPT_DUAL (1<<1)        /* this protocol uses two connections */\n#define PROTOPT_CLOSEACTION (1<<2) /* need action before socket close */\n/* some protocols will have to call the underlying functions without regard to\n   what exact state the socket signals. IE even if the socket says \"readable\",\n   the send function might need to be called while uploading, or vice versa.\n*/\n#define PROTOPT_DIRLOCK (1<<3)\n#define PROTOPT_NONETWORK (1<<4)   /* protocol doesn't use the network! */\n#define PROTOPT_NEEDSPWD (1<<5)    /* needs a password, and if none is set it\n                                      gets a default */\n#define PROTOPT_NOURLQUERY (1<<6)   /* protocol can't handle\n                                        url query strings (?foo=bar) ! */\n#define PROTOPT_CREDSPERREQUEST (1<<7) /* requires login credentials per\n                                          request instead of per connection */\n#define PROTOPT_ALPN (1<<8) /* set ALPN for this */\n/* (1<<9) was PROTOPT_STREAM, now free */\n#define PROTOPT_URLOPTIONS (1<<10) /* allow options part in the userinfo field\n                                      of the URL */\n#define PROTOPT_PROXY_AS_HTTP (1<<11) /* allow this non-HTTP scheme over a\n                                         HTTP proxy as HTTP proxies may know\n                                         this protocol and act as a gateway */\n#define PROTOPT_WILDCARD (1<<12) /* protocol supports wildcard matching */\n#define PROTOPT_USERPWDCTRL (1<<13) /* Allow \"control bytes\" (< 32 ascii) in\n                                       user name and password */\n#define PROTOPT_NOTCPPROXY (1<<14) /* this protocol can't proxy over TCP */\n\n#define CONNCHECK_NONE 0                 /* No checks */\n#define CONNCHECK_ISDEAD (1<<0)          /* Check if the connection is dead. */\n#define CONNCHECK_KEEPALIVE (1<<1)       /* Perform any keepalive function. */\n\n#define CONNRESULT_NONE 0                /* No extra information. */\n#define CONNRESULT_DEAD (1<<0)           /* The connection is dead. */\n\nstruct proxy_info {\n  struct hostname host;\n  int port;\n  unsigned char proxytype; /* curl_proxytype: what kind of proxy that is in\n                              use */\n  char *user;    /* proxy user name string, allocated */\n  char *passwd;  /* proxy password string, allocated */\n};\n\nstruct ldapconninfo;\n\n#define TRNSPRT_TCP 3\n#define TRNSPRT_UDP 4\n#define TRNSPRT_QUIC 5\n#define TRNSPRT_UNIX 6\n\n/*\n * The connectdata struct contains all fields and variables that should be\n * unique for an entire connection.\n */\nstruct connectdata {\n  struct Curl_llist_element bundle_node; /* conncache */\n\n  /* chunk is for HTTP chunked encoding, but is in the general connectdata\n     struct only because we can do just about any protocol through an HTTP\n     proxy and an HTTP proxy may in fact respond using chunked encoding */\n  struct Curl_chunker chunk;\n\n  curl_closesocket_callback fclosesocket; /* function closing the socket(s) */\n  void *closesocket_client;\n\n  /* This is used by the connection cache logic. If this returns TRUE, this\n     handle is still used by one or more easy handles and can only used by any\n     other easy handle without careful consideration (== only for\n     multiplexing) and it cannot be used by another multi handle! */\n#define CONN_INUSE(c) ((c)->easyq.size)\n\n  /**** Fields set when inited and not modified again */\n  curl_off_t connection_id; /* Contains a unique number to make it easier to\n                               track the connections in the log output */\n\n  /* 'dns_entry' is the particular host we use. This points to an entry in the\n     DNS cache and it will not get pruned while locked. It gets unlocked in\n     multi_done(). This entry will be NULL if the connection is reused as then\n     there is no name resolve done. */\n  struct Curl_dns_entry *dns_entry;\n\n  /* 'remote_addr' is the particular IP we connected to. it is owned, set\n   * and NULLed by the connected socket filter (if there is one). */\n  const struct Curl_sockaddr_ex *remote_addr;\n\n  struct hostname host;\n  char *hostname_resolve; /* host name to resolve to address, allocated */\n  char *secondaryhostname; /* secondary socket host name (ftp) */\n  struct hostname conn_to_host; /* the host to connect to. valid only if\n                                   bits.conn_to_host is set */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  struct proxy_info socks_proxy;\n  struct proxy_info http_proxy;\n#endif\n  /* 'primary_ip' and 'primary_port' get filled with peer's numerical\n     ip address and port number whenever an outgoing connection is\n     *attempted* from the primary socket to a remote address. When more\n     than one address is tried for a connection these will hold data\n     for the last attempt. When the connection is actually established\n     these are updated with data which comes directly from the socket. */\n\n  char primary_ip[MAX_IPADR_LEN];\n  char *user;    /* user name string, allocated */\n  char *passwd;  /* password string, allocated */\n  char *options; /* options string, allocated */\n  char *sasl_authzid;     /* authorization identity string, allocated */\n  char *oauth_bearer; /* OAUTH2 bearer, allocated */\n  struct curltime now;     /* \"current\" time */\n  struct curltime created; /* creation time */\n  struct curltime lastused; /* when returned to the connection cache */\n  curl_socket_t sock[2]; /* two sockets, the second is used for the data\n                            transfer when doing FTP */\n  Curl_recv *recv[2];\n  Curl_send *send[2];\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cfilter[2]; /* connection filters */\n\n  struct ssl_primary_config ssl_config;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  struct ssl_primary_config proxy_ssl_config;\n#endif\n  struct ConnectBits bits;    /* various state-flags for this connection */\n\n  const struct Curl_handler *handler; /* Connection's protocol handler */\n  const struct Curl_handler *given;   /* The protocol first given */\n\n  /* Protocols can use a custom keepalive mechanism to keep connections alive.\n     This allows those protocols to track the last time the keepalive mechanism\n     was used on this connection. */\n  struct curltime keepalive;\n\n  /**** curl_get() phase fields */\n\n  curl_socket_t sockfd;   /* socket to read from or CURL_SOCKET_BAD */\n  curl_socket_t writesockfd; /* socket to write to, it may very\n                                well be the same we read from.\n                                CURL_SOCKET_BAD disables */\n\n#ifdef HAVE_GSSAPI\n  BIT(sec_complete); /* if Kerberos is enabled for this connection */\n  unsigned char command_prot; /* enum protection_level */\n  unsigned char data_prot; /* enum protection_level */\n  unsigned char request_data_prot; /* enum protection_level */\n  size_t buffer_size;\n  struct krb5buffer in_buffer;\n  void *app_data;\n  const struct Curl_sec_client_mech *mech;\n  struct sockaddr_in local_addr;\n#endif\n\n#if defined(USE_KERBEROS5)    /* Consider moving some of the above GSS-API */\n  struct kerberos5data krb5;  /* variables into the structure definition, */\n#endif                        /* however, some of them are ftp specific. */\n\n  struct Curl_llist easyq;    /* List of easy handles using this connection */\n  curl_seek_callback seek_func; /* function that seeks the input */\n  void *seek_client;            /* pointer to pass to the seek() above */\n\n  /*************** Request - specific items ************/\n#if defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI) && defined(SECPKG_ATTR_ENDPOINT_BINDINGS)\n  CtxtHandle *sslContext;\n#endif\n\n#ifdef USE_GSASL\n  struct gsasldata gsasl;\n#endif\n\n#if defined(USE_NTLM)\n  curlntlm http_ntlm_state;\n  curlntlm proxy_ntlm_state;\n\n  struct ntlmdata ntlm;     /* NTLM differs from other authentication schemes\n                               because it authenticates connections, not\n                               single requests! */\n  struct ntlmdata proxyntlm; /* NTLM data for proxy */\n#endif\n\n#ifdef USE_SPNEGO\n  curlnegotiate http_negotiate_state;\n  curlnegotiate proxy_negotiate_state;\n\n  struct negotiatedata negotiate; /* state data for host Negotiate auth */\n  struct negotiatedata proxyneg; /* state data for proxy Negotiate auth */\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n  /* for chunked-encoded trailer */\n  struct dynbuf trailer;\n#endif\n\n  union {\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n    struct ftp_conn ftpc;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_SSH\n    struct ssh_conn sshc;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_TFTP\n    struct tftp_state_data *tftpc;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_IMAP\n    struct imap_conn imapc;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_POP3\n    struct pop3_conn pop3c;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_SMTP\n    struct smtp_conn smtpc;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_RTSP\n    struct rtsp_conn rtspc;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_SMB\n    struct smb_conn smbc;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_LIBRTMP\n    void *rtmp;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_OPENLDAP\n    struct ldapconninfo *ldapc;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_MQTT\n    struct mqtt_conn mqtt;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_WEBSOCKETS\n    struct websocket *ws;\n#endif\n    unsigned int unused:1; /* avoids empty union */\n  } proto;\n\n  struct connectbundle *bundle; /* The bundle we are member of */\n#ifdef USE_UNIX_SOCKETS\n  char *unix_domain_socket;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_HYPER\n  /* if set, an alternative data transfer function */\n  Curl_datastream datastream;\n#endif\n  /* When this connection is created, store the conditions for the local end\n     bind. This is stored before the actual bind and before any connection is\n     made and will serve the purpose of being used for comparison reasons so\n     that subsequent bound-requested connections aren't accidentally reusing\n     wrong connections. */\n  char *localdev;\n  unsigned short localportrange;\n  int waitfor;      /* current READ/WRITE bits to wait for */\n#if defined(HAVE_GSSAPI) || defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI)\n  int socks5_gssapi_enctype;\n#endif\n  /* The field below gets set in connect.c:connecthost() */\n  int port;        /* which port to use locally - to connect to */\n  int remote_port; /* the remote port, not the proxy port! */\n  int conn_to_port; /* the remote port to connect to. valid only if\n                       bits.conn_to_port is set */\n#ifdef ENABLE_IPV6\n  unsigned int scope_id;  /* Scope id for IPv6 */\n#endif\n  unsigned short localport;\n  unsigned short secondary_port; /* secondary socket remote port to connect to\n                                    (ftp) */\n  unsigned char cselect_bits; /* bitmask of socket events */\n  unsigned char alpn; /* APLN TLS negotiated protocol, a CURL_HTTP_VERSION*\n                         value */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  unsigned char proxy_alpn; /* APLN of proxy tunnel, CURL_HTTP_VERSION* */\n#endif\n  unsigned char transport; /* one of the TRNSPRT_* defines */\n  unsigned char ip_version; /* copied from the Curl_easy at creation time */\n  unsigned char httpversion; /* the HTTP version*10 reported by the server */\n  unsigned char connect_only;\n  unsigned char gssapi_delegation; /* inherited from set.gssapi_delegation */\n};\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n#define CURL_CONN_HOST_DISPNAME(c) \\\n          ((c)->bits.socksproxy ? (c)->socks_proxy.host.dispname : \\\n            (c)->bits.httpproxy ? (c)->http_proxy.host.dispname : \\\n              (c)->bits.conn_to_host ? (c)->conn_to_host.dispname : \\\n                (c)->host.dispname)\n#else\n#define CURL_CONN_HOST_DISPNAME(c) \\\n          (c)->bits.conn_to_host ? (c)->conn_to_host.dispname : \\\n            (c)->host.dispname\n#endif\n\n/* The end of connectdata. */\n\n/*\n * Struct to keep statistical and informational data.\n * All variables in this struct must be initialized/reset in Curl_initinfo().\n */\nstruct PureInfo {\n  int httpcode;  /* Recent HTTP, FTP, RTSP or SMTP response code */\n  int httpproxycode; /* response code from proxy when received separate */\n  int httpversion; /* the http version number X.Y = X*10+Y */\n  time_t filetime; /* If requested, this is might get set. Set to -1 if the\n                      time was unretrievable. */\n  curl_off_t request_size; /* the amount of bytes sent in the request(s) */\n  unsigned long proxyauthavail; /* what proxy auth types were announced */\n  unsigned long httpauthavail;  /* what host auth types were announced */\n  long numconnects; /* how many new connection did libcurl created */\n  char *contenttype; /* the content type of the object */\n  char *wouldredirect; /* URL this would've been redirected to if asked to */\n  curl_off_t retry_after; /* info from Retry-After: header */\n  unsigned int header_size;  /* size of read header(s) in bytes */\n\n  /* PureInfo members 'conn_primary_ip', 'conn_primary_port', 'conn_local_ip'\n     and, 'conn_local_port' are copied over from the connectdata struct in\n     order to allow curl_easy_getinfo() to return this information even when\n     the session handle is no longer associated with a connection, and also\n     allow curl_easy_reset() to clear this information from the session handle\n     without disturbing information which is still alive, and that might be\n     reused, in the connection cache. */\n\n  char conn_primary_ip[MAX_IPADR_LEN];\n  int conn_primary_port; /* this is the destination port to the connection,\n                            which might have been a proxy */\n  int conn_remote_port;  /* this is the \"remote port\", which is the port\n                            number of the used URL, independent of proxy or\n                            not */\n  char conn_local_ip[MAX_IPADR_LEN];\n  int conn_local_port;\n  const char *conn_scheme;\n  unsigned int conn_protocol;\n  struct curl_certinfo certs; /* info about the certs. Asked for with\n                                 CURLOPT_CERTINFO / CURLINFO_CERTINFO */\n  CURLproxycode pxcode;\n  BIT(timecond);  /* set to TRUE if the time condition didn't match, which\n                     thus made the document NOT get fetched */\n};\n\n\nstruct Progress {\n  time_t lastshow; /* time() of the last displayed progress meter or NULL to\n                      force redraw at next call */\n  curl_off_t size_dl; /* total expected size */\n  curl_off_t size_ul; /* total expected size */\n  curl_off_t downloaded; /* transferred so far */\n  curl_off_t uploaded; /* transferred so far */\n\n  curl_off_t current_speed; /* uses the currently fastest transfer */\n\n  int width; /* screen width at download start */\n  int flags; /* see progress.h */\n\n  timediff_t timespent;\n\n  curl_off_t dlspeed;\n  curl_off_t ulspeed;\n\n  timediff_t t_nslookup;\n  timediff_t t_connect;\n  timediff_t t_appconnect;\n  timediff_t t_pretransfer;\n  timediff_t t_starttransfer;\n  timediff_t t_redirect;\n\n  struct curltime start;\n  struct curltime t_startsingle;\n  struct curltime t_startop;\n  struct curltime t_acceptdata;\n\n\n  /* upload speed limit */\n  struct curltime ul_limit_start;\n  curl_off_t ul_limit_size;\n  /* download speed limit */\n  struct curltime dl_limit_start;\n  curl_off_t dl_limit_size;\n\n#define CURR_TIME (5 + 1) /* 6 entries for 5 seconds */\n\n  curl_off_t speeder[ CURR_TIME ];\n  struct curltime speeder_time[ CURR_TIME ];\n  int speeder_c;\n  BIT(callback);  /* set when progress callback is used */\n  BIT(is_t_startransfer_set);\n};\n\ntypedef enum {\n    RTSPREQ_NONE, /* first in list */\n    RTSPREQ_OPTIONS,\n    RTSPREQ_DESCRIBE,\n    RTSPREQ_ANNOUNCE,\n    RTSPREQ_SETUP,\n    RTSPREQ_PLAY,\n    RTSPREQ_PAUSE,\n    RTSPREQ_TEARDOWN,\n    RTSPREQ_GET_PARAMETER,\n    RTSPREQ_SET_PARAMETER,\n    RTSPREQ_RECORD,\n    RTSPREQ_RECEIVE,\n    RTSPREQ_LAST /* last in list */\n} Curl_RtspReq;\n\nstruct auth {\n  unsigned long want;  /* Bitmask set to the authentication methods wanted by\n                          app (with CURLOPT_HTTPAUTH or CURLOPT_PROXYAUTH). */\n  unsigned long picked;\n  unsigned long avail; /* Bitmask for what the server reports to support for\n                          this resource */\n  BIT(done); /* TRUE if digest should be done IE-style or FALSE if it\n                   should be RFC compliant */\n};\n\n#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\nstruct Curl_data_prio_node {\n  struct Curl_data_prio_node *next;\n  struct Curl_easy *data;\n};\n#endif\n\n/**\n * Priority information for an easy handle in relation to others\n * on the same connection.\n * TODO: we need to adapt it to the new priority scheme as defined in RFC 9218\n */\nstruct Curl_data_priority {\n#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\n  /* tree like dependencies only implemented in nghttp2 */\n  struct Curl_easy *parent;\n  struct Curl_data_prio_node *children;\n#endif\n  int weight;\n#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\n  BIT(exclusive);\n#endif\n};\n\n/*\n * This struct is for holding data that was attempted to get sent to the user's\n * callback but is held due to pausing. Return CURLE_OK or CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY. */\n\n  Curl_safefree(*charp);\n\n  if(s) {\n    if(strlen(s) > CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n    *charp = strdup(s);\n    if(!*charp)\n      return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n  }\n\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nCURLcode Curl_setblobopt(struct curl_blob **blobp,\n                         const struct curl_blob *blob)\n{\n  /* free the previous storage at `blobp' and replace by a dynamic storage\n     copy of blob. If CURL_BLOB_COPY is set, the data is copied. */\n\n  Curl_safefree(*blobp);\n\n  if(blob) {\n    struct curl_blob *nblob;\n    if(blob->len > CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    nblob = (struct curl_blob *)\n      malloc(sizeof(struct curl_blob) +\n             ((blob->flags & CURL_BLOB_COPY) ? blob->len : 0));\n    if(!nblob)\n      return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n    *nblob = *blob;\n    if(blob->flags & CURL_BLOB_COPY) {\n      /* put the data after the blob struct in memory */\n      nblob->data = (char *)nblob + sizeof(struct curl_blob);\n      memcpy(nblob->data, blob->data, blob->len);\n    }\n\n    *blobp = nblob;\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  }\n\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode setstropt_userpwd(char *option, char **userp, char **passwdp)\n{\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  char *user = NULL;\n  char *passwd = NULL;\n\n  /* Parse the login details if specified. It not then we treat NULL as a hint\n     to clear the existing data */\n  if(option) {\n    size_t len = strlen(option);\n    if(len > CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n    result = Curl_parse_login_details(option, len,\n                                      (userp ? &user : NULL),\n                                      (passwdp ? &passwd : NULL),\n                                      NULL);\n  }\n\n  if(!result) {\n    /* Store the username part of option if required */\n    if(userp) {\n      if(!user && option && option[0] == ':') {\n        /* Allocate an empty string instead of returning NULL as user name */\n        user = strdup(\"\");\n        if(!user)\n          result = CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n      }\n\n      Curl_safefree(*userp);\n      *userp = user;\n    }\n\n    /* Store the password part of option if required */\n    if(passwdp) {\n      Curl_safefree(*passwdp);\n      *passwdp = passwd;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return result;\n}\n\n#define C_SSLVERSION_VALUE(x) (x & 0xffff)\n#define C_SSLVERSION_MAX_VALUE(x) (x & 0xffff0000)\n\nstatic CURLcode protocol2num(const char *str, curl_prot_t *val)\n{\n  if(!str)\n    return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n  if(curl_strequal(str, \"all\")) {\n    *val = ~(curl_prot_t) 0;\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  }\n\n  *val = 0;\n\n  do {\n    const char *token = str;\n    size_t tlen;\n\n    str = strchr(str, ',');\n    tlen = str? (size_t) (str - token): strlen(token);\n    if(tlen) {\n      const struct Curl_handler *h = Curl_builtin_scheme(token, tlen);\n\n      if(!h)\n        return CURLE_UNSUPPORTED_PROTOCOL;\n\n      *val |= h->protocol;\n    }\n  } while(str && str++);\n\n  if(!*val)\n    /* no protocol listed */\n    return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\n/*\n * Do not make Curl_vsetopt() static: it is called from\n * packages/OS400/ccsidcurl.c.\n */\nCURLcode Curl_vsetopt(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLoption option, va_list param)\n{\n  char *argptr;\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  long arg;\n  unsigned long uarg;\n  curl_off_t bigsize;\n\n  switch(option) {\n  case CURLOPT_DNS_CACHE_TIMEOUT:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < -1)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else if(arg > INT_MAX)\n      arg = INT_MAX;\n\n    data->set.dns_cache_timeout = (int)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_CA_CACHE_TIMEOUT:\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < -1)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    else if(arg > INT_MAX)\n      arg = INT_MAX;\n\n    data->set.general_ssl.ca_cache_timeout = (int)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_DNS_USE_GLOBAL_CACHE:\n    /* deprecated */\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SSL_CIPHER_LIST:\n    /* set a list of cipher we want to use in the SSL connection */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_CIPHER_LIST],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSL_CIPHER_LIST:\n    /* set a list of cipher we want to use in the SSL connection for proxy */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_CIPHER_LIST_PROXY],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_TLS13_CIPHERS:\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_TLS13_CIPHERSUITES)) {\n      /* set preferred list of TLS 1.3 cipher suites */\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_CIPHER13_LIST],\n                              va_arg(param, char *));\n    }\n    else\n      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_TLS13_CIPHERS:\n    if(Curl_ssl_supports(data, SSLSUPP_TLS13_CIPHERSUITES)) {\n      /* set preferred list of TLS 1.3 cipher suites for proxy */\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_SSL_CIPHER13_LIST_PROXY],\n                              va_arg(param, char *));\n    }\n    else\n      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_RANDOM_FILE:\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_EGDSOCKET:\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_MAXCONNECTS:\n    /*\n     * Set the absolute number of maximum simultaneous alive connection that\n     * libcurl is allowed to have.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.maxconnects = arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FORBID_REUSE:\n    /*\n     * When this transfer is done, it must not be left to be reused by a\n     * subsequent transfer but shall be closed immediately.\n     */\n    data->set.reuse_forbid = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FRESH_CONNECT:\n    /*\n     * This transfer shall not use a previously cached connection but\n     * should be made with a fresh new connect!\n     */\n    data->set.reuse_fresh = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_VERBOSE:\n    /*\n     * Verbose means infof() calls that give a lot of information about\n     * the connection and transfer procedures as well as internal choices.\n     */\n    data->set.verbose = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_HEADER:\n    /*\n     * Set to include the header in the general data output stream.\n     */\n    data->set.include_header = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_NOPROGRESS:\n    /*\n     * Shut off the internal supported progress meter\n     */\n    data->set.hide_progress = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    if(data->set.hide_progress)\n      data->progress.flags |= PGRS_HIDE;\n    else\n      data->progress.flags &= ~PGRS_HIDE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_NOBODY:\n    /*\n     * Do not include the body part in the output data stream.\n     */\n    data->set.opt_no_body = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n    if(data->set.opt_no_body)\n      /* in HTTP lingo, no body means using the HEAD request... */\n      data->set.method = HTTPREQ_HEAD;\n    else if(data->set.method == HTTPREQ_HEAD)\n      data->set.method = HTTPREQ_GET;\n#endif\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FAILONERROR:\n    /*\n     * Don't output the >=400 error code HTML-page, but instead only\n     * return error.\n     */\n    data->set.http_fail_on_error = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_KEEP_SENDING_ON_ERROR:\n    data->set.http_keep_sending_on_error = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ?\n      TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_UPLOAD:\n  case CURLOPT_PUT:\n    /*\n     * We want to sent data to the remote host. If this is HTTP, that equals\n     * using the PUT request.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg) {\n      /* If this is HTTP, PUT is what's needed to \"upload\" */\n      data->set.method = HTTPREQ_PUT;\n      data->set.opt_no_body = FALSE; /* this is implied */\n    }\n    else\n      /* In HTTP, the opposite of upload is GET (unless NOBODY is true as\n         then this can be changed to HEAD later on) */\n      data->set.method = HTTPREQ_GET;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_REQUEST_TARGET:\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_TARGET],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_FILETIME:\n    /*\n     * Try to get the file time of the remote document. The time will\n     * later (possibly) become available using curl_easy_getinfo().\n     */\n    data->set.get_filetime = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_SERVER_RESPONSE_TIMEOUT:\n    /*\n     * Option that specifies how quickly a server response must be obtained\n     * before it is considered failure. For pingpong protocols.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg >= 0) && (arg <= (INT_MAX/1000)))\n      data->set.server_response_timeout = (unsigned int)arg * 1000;\n    else\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    break;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_TFTP\n  case CURLOPT_TFTP_NO_OPTIONS:\n    /*\n     * Option that prevents libcurl from sending TFTP option requests to the\n     * server.\n     */\n    data->set.tftp_no_options = va_arg(param, long) != 0;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TFTP_BLKSIZE:\n    /*\n     * TFTP option that specifies the block size to use for data transmission.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < 0)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.tftp_blksize = arg;\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_NETRC\n  case CURLOPT_NETRC:\n    /*\n     * Parse the $HOME/.netrc file\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < CURL_NETRC_IGNORED) || (arg >= CURL_NETRC_LAST))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.use_netrc = (unsigned char)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_NETRC_FILE:\n    /*\n     * Use this file instead of the $HOME/.netrc file\n     */\n    result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_NETRC_FILE],\n                            va_arg(param, char *));\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURLOPT_TRANSFERTEXT:\n    /*\n     * This option was previously named 'FTPASCII'. Renamed to work with\n     * more protocols than merely FTP.\n     *\n     * Transfer using ASCII (instead of BINARY).\n     */\n    data->set.prefer_ascii = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TIMECONDITION:\n    /*\n     * Set HTTP time condition. This must be one of the defines in the\n     * curl/curl.h header file.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if((arg < CURL_TIMECOND_NONE) || (arg >= CURL_TIMECOND_LAST))\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.timecondition = (unsigned char)(curl_TimeCond)arg;\n    break;\n  case CURLOPT_TIMEVALUE:\n    /*\n     * This is the value to compare with the remote document with the\n     * method set with CURLOPT_TIMECONDITION\n     */\n    data->set.timevalue = (time_t)va_arg(param, long);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_TIMEVALUE_LARGE:\n    /*\n     * This is the value to compare with the remote document with the\n     * method set with CURLOPT_TIMECONDITION\n     */\n    data->set.timevalue = (time_t)va_arg(param, curl_off_t);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_SSLVERSION:\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  case CURLOPT_PROXY_SSLVERSION:\n#endif\n    /*\n     * Set explicit SSL version to try to connect with, as some SSL\n     * implementations are lame.\n     */\n#ifdef USE_SSL\n    {\n      long version, version_max;\n      struct ssl_primary_config *primary = &data->set.ssl.primary;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n      if(option != CURLOPT_SSLVERSION)\n        primary = &data->set.proxy_ssl.primary;\n#endif\n\n      arg = va_arg(param, long);\n\n      version = C_SSLVERSION_VALUE(arg);\n      version_max = C_SSLVERSION_MAX_VALUE(arg);\n\n      if(version < CURL_SSLVERSION_DEFAULT ||\n         version == CURL_SSLVERSION_SSLv2 ||\n         version == CURL_SSLVERSION_SSLv3 ||\n         version >= CURL_SSLVERSION_LAST ||\n         version_max < CURL_SSLVERSION_MAX_NONE ||\n         version_max >= CURL_SSLVERSION_MAX_LAST)\n        return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n      primary->version = (unsigned char)version;\n      primary->version_max = (unsigned int)version_max;\n    }\n#else\n    result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n#endif\n    break;\n\n    /* MQTT \"borrows\" some of the HTTP options */\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_HTTP) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_MQTT)\n  case CURLOPT_COPYPOSTFIELDS:\n    /*\n     * A string with POST data. Makes curl HTTP POST. Even if it is NULL.\n     * If needed, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDSIZE must have been set prior to\n     *  CURLOPT_COPYPOSTFIELDS and not altered later.\n     */\n    argptr = va_arg(param, char *);\n\n    if(!argptr || data->set.postfieldsize == -1)\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS], argptr);\n    else {\n      /*\n       *  Check that requested length does not overflow the size_t type.\n       */\n\n      if((data->set.postfieldsize < 0) ||\n         ((sizeof(curl_off_t) != sizeof(size_t)) &&\n          (data->set.postfieldsize > (curl_off_t)((size_t)-1))))\n        result = CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n      else {\n        char *p;\n\n        (void) Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS], NULL);\n\n        /* Allocate even when size == 0. This satisfies the need of possible\n           later address compare to detect the COPYPOSTFIELDS mode, and\n           to mark that postfields is used rather than read function or\n           form data.\n        */\n        p = malloc((size_t)(data->set.postfieldsize?\n                            data->set.postfieldsize:1));\n\n        if(!p)\n          result = CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n        else {\n          if(data->set.postfieldsize)\n            memcpy(p, argptr, (size_t)data->set.postfieldsize);\n\n          data->set.str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS] = p;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    data->set.postfields = data->set.str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS];\n    data->set.method = HTTPREQ_POST;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS:\n    /*\n     * Like above, but use static data instead of copying it.\n     */\n    data->set.postfields = va_arg(param, void *);\n    /* Release old copied data. */\n    (void) Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS], NULL);\n    data->set.method = HTTPREQ_POST;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_POSTFIELDSIZE:\n    /*\n     * The size of the POSTFIELD data to prevent libcurl to do strlen() to\n     * figure it out. Enables binary posts.\n     */\n    bigsize = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(bigsize < -1)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n    if(data->set.postfieldsize < bigsize &&\n       data->set.postfields == data->set.str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS]) {\n      /* Previous CURLOPT_COPYPOSTFIELDS is no longer valid. */\n      (void) Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS], NULL);\n      data->set.postfields = NULL;\n    }\n\n    data->set.postfieldsize = bigsize;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_POSTFIELDSIZE_LARGE:\n    /*\n     * The size of the POSTFIELD data to prevent libcurl to do strlen() to\n     * figure it out. Enables binary posts.\n     */\n    bigsize = va_arg(param, curl_off_t);\n    if(bigsize < -1)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n\n    if(data->set.postfieldsize < bigsize &&\n       data->set.postfields == data->set.str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS]) {\n      /* Previous CURLOPT_COPYPOSTFIELDS is no longer valid. */\n      (void) Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_COPYPOSTFIELDS], NULL);\n      data->set.postfields = NULL;\n    }\n\n    data->set.postfieldsize = bigsize;\n    break;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n  case CURLOPT_AUTOREFERER:\n    /*\n     * Switch on automatic referer that gets set if curl follows locations.\n     */\n    data->set.http_auto_referer = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_ACCEPT_ENCODING:\n    /*\n     * String to use at the value of Accept-Encoding header.\n     *\n     * If the encoding is set to \"\" we use an Accept-Encoding header that\n     * encompasses all the encodings we support.\n     * If the encoding is set to NULL we don't send an Accept-Encoding header\n     * and ignore an received Content-Encoding header.\n     *\n     */\n    argptr = va_arg(param, char *);\n    if(argptr && !*argptr) {\n      argptr = Curl_all_content_encodings();\n      if(!argptr)\n        result = CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n      else {\n        result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_ENCODING], argptr);\n        free(argptr);\n      }\n    }\n    else\n      result = Curl_setstropt(&data->set.str[STRING_ENCODING], argptr);\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_TRANSFER_ENCODING:\n    data->set.http_transfer_encoding = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ?\n      TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_FOLLOWLOCATION:\n    /*\n     * Follow Location: header hints on an HTTP-server.\n     */\n    data->set.http_follow_location = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_UNRESTRICTED_AUTH:\n    /*\n     * Send authentication (user+password) when following locations, even when\n     * hostname changed.\n     */\n    data->set.allow_auth_to_other_hosts =\n      (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_MAXREDIRS:\n    /*\n     * The maximum amount of hops you allow curl to follow Location:\n     * headers. This should mostly be used to detect never-ending loops.\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < -1)\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.maxredirs = arg;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_POSTREDIR:\n    /*\n     * Set the behavior of POST when redirecting\n     * CURL_REDIR_GET_ALL - POST is changed to GET after 301 and 302\n     * CURL_REDIR_POST_301 - POST is kept as POST after 301\n     * CURL_REDIR_POST_302 - POST is kept as POST after 302\n     * CURL_REDIR_POST_303 - POST is kept as POST after 303\n     * CURL_REDIR_POST_ALL - POST is kept as POST after 301, 302 and 303\n     * other - POST is kept as POST after 301 and 302\n     */\n    arg = va_arg(param, long);\n    if(arg < CURL_REDIR_GET_ALL)\n      /* no return error on too high numbers since the bitmask could be\n         extended in a future */\n      return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n    data->set.keep_post = arg & CURL_REDIR_POST_ALL;\n    break;\n\n  case CURLOPT_POST:\n    /* Does this option serve a purpose anymore? Yes it does, when\n       CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS isn't used and the POST data is read off the\n       callback! */\n    if(va_arg(param, long)) {\n      data->set.method = HTTPREQ_POST;\n      data->set.opt_no_body = FALSE; /* this is implied */\n    }\n    else\n      data->set.method = HTTPREQ_GET;\n    break;\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FORM_API\n  case CURLOPT_HTTPPOST:\n    /*\n     * Set to make us do HTTP POST.
